response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced generalist with genuine developer-tooling interest — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack requirements are largely unmet. Java appears only briefly and peripherally, there is no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise-framework experience, and no evidence of deep JVM expertise, which are the central pillars of this role.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored developer tools (test runner, assertion library)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Proven experience with complex, high-scale systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure — no deep JVM knowledge demonstrated
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework background
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node full-stack and AI tooling, far from enterprise JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
